cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what advice Wesla Whitfield would most likely give other disabled people, based on the passage provided. Let me go through the passage again to make sure I catch the right details. So, the passage describes Wesla Whitfield's story. She became paralyzed from the waist down after a shooting when she was 29. Initially, she struggled with depression and even tried to kill herself. But after being in the hospital, she recovered. She mentions that the only thing she lost was the ability to walk, but she still has her mind, which she considers her most valuable asset. She points to her head and says, "Everything important is in here. The only real disability in life is losing your mind." She also mentions that when she feels frustrated because others are dancing and she can't, she removes herself to focus on what she can do.
